rawMetadata: {"compiler":{"version":"0.8.27+commit.40a35a09"},"language":"Solidity","output":{"abi":[{"inputs":[{"internalType":"address","name":"owner_","type":"address"},{"internalType":"address","name":"v12Runtime_","type":"address"},{"internalType":"address","name":"router_","type":"address"}],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"constructor"},{"inputs":[],"name":"EmptyProgram","type":"error"},{"inputs":[],"name":"NotOwner","type":"error"},{"stateMutability":"payable","type":"fallback"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"bytes[]","name":"ingredients","type":"bytes[]"}],"name":"cook","outputs":[{"internalType":"bytes","name":"","type":"bytes"}],"stateMutability":"payable","type":"function"},{"inputs":[],"name":"owner","outputs":[{"internalType":"address","name":"","type":"address"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"inputs":[],"name":"router","outputs":[{"internalType":"address","name":"","type":"address"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"inputs":[],"name":"v12Runtime","outputs":[{"internalType":"address","name":"","type":"address"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"stateMutability":"payable","type":"receive"}],"devdoc":{"details":"A V12Pot is the v12 counterpart of {Pot}. It is deployed deterministically by the      {V12Kitchen}, one (or more, via salt) per owner. It differs from {Pot} in that execution      is split across TWO implementations instead of one:        - `v12Runtime` \u2014 the standalone v12 Huff interpreter. It has no swap Router; it is a pure          bytecode VM. {cook} delegatecalls into it.        - `router` \u2014 the v1 {Router} implementation (the bare DEX swap surface: `swap`, `swapV3`,          `swapV4`, `swapInfinity*`, plus the V3/V4/Algebra/Maverick reentrant pool callbacks). The          {fallback} delegatecalls into it. This is the {Router} directly, NOT the v1 {SauceRouter}          (which layers the v1 Sauce VM on top) \u2014 a V12Pot runs the v12 Huff runtime as its VM, so          it needs only the swap surface.      Because both run as delegatecalls in THIS Pot's context, `address(this)`, balances, storage,      approvals and transient storage all resolve to this Pot. A v12 program that wants to swap      issues a CALL targeting `address(this).swapV3(...)`; that re-enters this contract, misses the      `cook` selector, and lands in {fallback}, which delegatecalls the v1 Router \u2014 so the v12      program and the swap logic share one execution context.      The split preserves the v1 swap invariants verbatim:        - The Router's swap entrypoints are `onlySelf` \u2014 they require `msg.sender == address(this)`.          The v12 program's swap CALL targets `address.self`, so the self-call satisfies this.        - Each pool callback verifies `msg.sender` against an expected pool address set in transient          storage before the swap. Every frame here is a delegatecall into the Pot, so the Router's          tstore/tload context is shared across the swap and its callback.      The fallback is an open forwarder by design (same rationale as {Pot}): it carries the swap      self-calls and pool callbacks, and the Router guards fund movement itself.","kind":"dev","methods":{"cook(bytes[])":{"details":"Signature mirrors ISauce.cook (bytes[] ingredients -> bytes returnData). Unlike {Pot},      the calldata is NOT forwarded verbatim: the v12 runtime reads its program from calldata at      offset 0 with NO function selector and NO ABI wrapper, so forwarding the abi-encoded      `cook(bytes[])` calldata would make the interpreter execute the ABI header bytes as      opcodes. Instead, the raw program blob is `ingredients[0]` (v12 emits exactly one blob),      and it is delegatecalled into the runtime as the ENTIRE calldata.      Callable by the owner or by the Pot itself, with the same rationale as {Pot.cook}: a      self-call can only originate from inside an in-flight cook the owner authorised."}},"title":"V12Pot","version":1},"userdoc":{"kind":"user","methods":{"cook(bytes[])":{"notice":"Execute a v12 sauce program in this Pot's context."},"owner()":{"notice":"Addresses allowed to start execution in this Pot: the owner EOA, or the Pot itself         (the sauce VM uses CALL targeting `address(this)`; those land back here with         `msg.sender == address(this)`)."},"router()":{"notice":"The v1 {Router} DEX swap implementation that {fallback} delegatecalls into."},"v12Runtime()":{"notice":"The v12 Huff interpreter that {cook} delegatecalls into."}},"notice":"Per-user proxy that holds funds and executes v12 (postfix) sauce bytecode in its own         context, while still reaching the v1 swap surface for pool interactions.","version":1}},"settings":{"compilationTarget":{"src/V12Pot.sol":"V12Pot"},"evmVersion":"cancun","libraries":{},"metadata":{"bytecodeHash":"ipfs"},"optimizer":{"enabled":true,"runs":1000},"remappings":[":@openzeppelin/contracts/=lib/openzeppelin-contracts/contracts/",":forge-std/=lib/forge-std/src/",":foundry-huff-neo/=lib/foundry-huff-neo/src/",":openzeppelin-contracts/=lib/openzeppelin-contracts/"],"viaIR":true},"sources":{"src/V12Pot.sol":{"keccak256":"0x534133319b0574fe061983f20f4399c7875154f320d3bd02483f7eafee8b95ab","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://0b6a3357c30dc522ea8e37d6d5ac6f8174620bd27b254cb906a6e800a0eb905a","dweb:/ipfs/QmaYxCkE8Vm5q3gkwWh8pirbWcbmAefDgcriKezfD82QS9"]}},"version":1}

cook(bytes[]).details: Signature mirrors ISauce.cook (bytes[] ingredients -> bytes returnData). Unlike {Pot},      the calldata is NOT forwarded verbatim: the v12 runtime reads its program from calldata at      offset 0 with NO function selector and NO ABI wrapper, so forwarding the abi-encoded      `cook(bytes[])` calldata would make the interpreter execute the ABI header bytes as      opcodes. Instead, the raw program blob is `ingredients[0]` (v12 emits exactly one blob),      and it is delegatecalled into the runtime as the ENTIRE calldata.      Callable by the owner or by the Pot itself, with the same rationale as {Pot.cook}: a      self-call can only originate from inside an in-flight cook the owner authorised.

cook(bytes[]).notice: Execute a v12 sauce program in this Pot's context.

owner().notice: Addresses allowed to start execution in this Pot: the owner EOA, or the Pot itself         (the sauce VM uses CALL targeting `address(this)`; those land back here with         `msg.sender == address(this)`).

router().notice: The v1 {Router} DEX swap implementation that {fallback} delegatecalls into.

v12Runtime().notice: The v12 Huff interpreter that {cook} delegatecalls into.
